data_summary: Global residential LPG demand is projected to reach 180 mtpa by 2030, driven by clean cooking in Asia and Africa.
LPG consumption for clean cooking in Sub-Saharan Africa is expected to grow 8% annually; Nigeria, Kenya, and Ghana lead.
India and Indonesia dominate residential LPG demand; PMUY and 3kg programmes continue to drive penetration.
BioLPG and rLPG are gaining share in off-grid heating; EU taxonomy and RED III will shape demand.
MENA remains the largest LPG export region; Saudi Arabia and UAE are key suppliers to Asia and Africa.
US LPG exports set new records; domestic demand stable with growth in renewable LPG and RINs.
Brazil and Argentina lead LPG demand in LatAm; biogas and BioLPG projects are expanding.
Autogas demand varies by region; Turkey and South Korea remain top markets; electrification is a long-term headwind.
Industrial LPG use in petrochemicals (PDH) and process heat continues to grow, especially in China and Middle East.
